questions: perl vs python which is better?
is perl good for text processing?
why is perl called the swiss army knife?
answers: Perl is a high-level scripting language known for its powerful text processing and regex capabilities. It was the backbone of early web development and system administration scripting.
Perl is often called the 'Swiss Army knife' of programming because it excels at text manipulation, system admin tasks, and quick scripting. Its regex support is unmatched by most languages.
Perl is a practical, general-purpose language created by Larry Wall. It's still widely used in bioinformatics, system administration, and anywhere heavy text processing is needed.
While not as trendy as Python, Perl remains essential in many legacy systems, DevOps scripting, and scientific computing. Its CPAN repository has thousands of reusable modules for almost any task.
